full_text:
Logic table fine-tuning meeting 2023. 06.08.

- TMA data is not normalized

- For pre and post we can choose the samples we want to use

- One possible review question: do it on colorectal cancer (in this
 > case we can get data)

- For TMA data: we have to look at the images: If the new results are
 > better we can say it outperforms the old method (benchmarking but
 > better)

- In TMA there were some manual cluster annotations (Tribus can
 > correct manual cluster annotations) → New results can be right,
 > there might be less tumor cells

- We can look at the images

- We have to think how to put it (if we correct labels) so that it
 > doesn't imply that it would ruin results from the previous labels.
 > Annotation is never perfect!

- We have to find out if those cells are true or not, look at it in
 > Napari! (Previously lymphoid cells went to tumor)

- Possibly the change is too big: we have to check and change the
 > logic table a little

TMA table tips and ideas:

- For logic table: less strict -1 annotations (there might be
 > spillover with CK7 in lymphoid) → CK7 is 0 for the lymphoid, also
 > Ki67 (not necessarily use this one, it is a functional marker)

- Vimentin 0 in tumor: tumor cells can express vimentin as a marker
 > for EMT

- First Ki67, then Vimentin to change, do it one by one

- FoxP3 -1 everywhere except lymphoid

r)

- Vimentin 0 in tumor: tumor cells can express vimentin as a marker
 > for EMT

- First Ki67, then Vimentin to change, do it one by one

- FoxP3 -1 everywhere except lymphoid

- CD31 0 in myeloid, 1 in stromal and negative everywhere else

- CD20 negative in the myleoids (theoretically, maybe from staining it
 > has to be used a bit differently)

- FoxP3 is a functional marker in the lymphoids, try not using at all
 > on the global level (explain that since its a functional marker,
 > it's okay to use tt in the next level, one of the reasons for
 > using levels)

For Stellar:

- Myeloid mixes with epithelial

- Do we want to go deeper with annotation?

- Add a bit more data

- Apply it on other samples too (the ones which were annotated with
 > the algorithm)

- All the stroma can be merged

- We can focus on identifying macrophages, CD8, CD4 cells,
 > endothelials

- Merge enterocytes

- Let's try to do it on around 10 celltypes

- Limphatics can be included, pick nice ones (and we do the same
 > celltypes on both manually labelled and machine learning ones)
